def: s.m. Land belonging to a village furthest from the inhabitants.
s.m. Sand; dust; powder.
s.m. Talc, mica ( = bhoḍal).
v.t. = bhulānā, q.v.
s.f. = bhuṛarī, q.v.
s.f. Rage; resentment; spleen; animosity
subs: v.n. To vent one's rage or spleen.

ety: redupl. of S. भुर्, rt. भृ+कः
def: adj. (f. -ī), Dry, and in a state of powder, powdery; mealy, short (as bread), crisp, crackling; parched (as grain); (cf. būrā)
subs: (-par), To thirst (for), long (for), hanker (after), eagerly desire.

def: s.f. Powderiness; mealiness; shortness, crispness.
v.n. To throw or sprinkle sugar, salt, &c. (upon, -par); to long (for), &c. = bhurbhurā honā, q.v.s.v. bhurbhurā.
s.f. contrac. of bhurbhurāhaṭ, q.v.
s.f. = bhurbhurāhaṭ, q.v.; a light sandy soil.
s.f. Light, sandy soil = bhūṛ, q.v.
adj. & adv. Much, many, numerous, abundant; — much, abundantly, exceedingly, often, frequently, repeatedly; — s.m. An epithet of Brahmā; of Vishnu; of Śiva; gold; day
subs: adj. Possessing great splendour, splendid, bright, illustrious
adj. & s.m. Having much profit, very profitable; — great gain.

def: s.m. A species of birch (see bhoj).
s.m. The morning star (cf. bhor).
s.m. Smash; fragments, pieces, splinters, chips; husk, chaff: bhurkas nikālnā (-kā), To beat to shivers or smithers, to make mincemeat (of).
s.f. Hole, gap, rent (cf. bhurkas); hole or pit for water ( = bhuṛ); measure of land.
s.f. The earth; desert; sandy soil.
s.f. Spell of a jogī or ascetic (performed by scattering a handful of ashes over the head and repeating a spell or mantra).
s.f. Alms, &c. = bhūr, q.v.
s.m. Mash made of boiled or fried vegetables (the vegetables being broken or mashed in the hand after being boiled, &c.); mash, squash
subs: (-kā), bhurtā nikālnā (-kā), To make a mash (of), beat to a pulp, give a sound thrashing.

def: s.m. Husk (of com), straw; chaff, bran
subs: v.n. To make the chaff fly; to beat one to pieces, make mincemeat of; — to lament, mourn, bewail (as the Shīʻas do in Moḥarram)
v.n. To cause to be filled or stuffed with straw; to flay (a person), and fill the skin with straw and burn it (an oriental mode of punishment)
(-meṅ), To fill or stuff with straw, chaff, or or bran
s.f. lit. 'Assignment on chaff'; an assignment from which nothing can be obtained, a valueless assignment.

def: s.m. = bhus; bhūsā, qq.v.
s.m. Chaff or husk of corn, &c. = bhus, q.v.

r: bhusaiṛā, bhusaurī, bhusautā, bhusauṅdā, bhuserā, bhuselā, bhusehrā
def: A place where corn, straw, or chaff is kept.

r: bhūsaulā, bhūsaurī, bhūsehrā, bhusaurī, bhusaulā, bhusehrā
def: s.m. = H بهوسوري भूसौरी bhūsaurī, s.f. = H بهوسيہرا भूसेहरा bhūsehrā, s.m. Prop. bhusaurī, bhusaulā, bhusehrā, qq.v.

r: bhusauṅdā, bhusaurī, bhusautā, bhuserā, bhusaiṛā, bhuselā, bhusehrā
def: A place where corn, straw, or chaff is kept.

r: bhūsaurī, bhūsaulā, bhūsehrā, bhusaurī, bhusaulā, bhusehrā
def: s.f. = H بهوسولا भूसौला bhūsaulā, s.m. = H بهوسيہرا भूसेहरा bhūsehrā, s.m. Prop. bhusaurī, bhusaulā, bhusehrā, qq.v.

r: bhusaurī, bhusautā, bhusauṅdā, bhuserā, bhusaiṛā, bhuselā, bhusehrā
def: A place where corn, straw, or chaff is kept.

r: bhusautā, bhusaurī, bhusauṅdā, bhuserā, bhusaiṛā, bhuselā, bhusehrā
def: A place where corn, straw, or chaff is kept.
s.m. = bhusāwan, q.v.
s.m. Tax on boats freighted with grain.

r: bhūsehrā, bhūsaurī, bhūsaulā, bhusaurī, bhusaulā, bhusehrā
def: s.m. = H بهوسوري भूसौरी bhūsaurī, s.f. = H بهوسولا भूसौला bhūsaulā, s.m. Prop. bhusaurī, bhusaulā, bhusehrā, qq.v.

r: bhusehrā, bhusaurī, bhusautā, bhusauṅdā, bhuserā, bhusaiṛā, bhuselā
def: A place where corn, straw, or chaff is kept.

r: bhuselā, bhusaurī, bhusautā, bhusauṅdā, bhuserā, bhusaiṛā, bhusehrā
def: A place where corn, straw, or chaff is kept.

r: bhuserā, bhusaurī, bhusautā, bhusauṅdā, bhusaiṛā, bhuselā, bhusehrā
def: A place where corn, straw, or chaff is kept.
s.m. An adorning or decorating; ornament, decoration, embellishment, jewels, dress.
part. Adorned, decorated, embellished, ornamented, bejewelled, dressed.
s.f. Bran; chaff.
s.m. Hissing (of a snake = phuskār).
v.n. To hiss (as a snake = phus-kārnā).
v.n. To bark = bhūṅsna; bhauṅknā.
s.m. = bhusrā, q.v.
s.m. Inferior kind of wheat, ear that contains much chaff and little grain.
s.m. = bhuserā, bhuselā; qq.v.
s.f. A kind of weapon (apparently a kind of fire-arms); a small gun.
s.m. Epithet of a Brāhmaṇ.
s.m. = bhūt, q.v.
s.f. Land that does not retain water (cf. bhūṛ).

part. & s.m. Been, become, produced, formed, being, existing, existent; actually being, really happened, true; right, proper; past, gone, former; — a created being, child, son, youth; a great devotee or ascetic; a kind of demigod; a kind of malignant spirit (believed by the Hindūs to haunt graveyards, lurk in trees, animate carcases, and delude or devour human beings), evil spirit, demon, ghost, goblin, imp; a vicious or wicked person, a devil; an ugly or hideous-looking person; an epithet of Śiva; (in Gram.) the preterite tense; (in Philos.) an element (of which, according to the Hindūs, there are five, viz. earth, water, fire, air, ether); (in Law) the real state of the case, the matter of fact
subs: s.m. lit. 'Composed of the five elements'; an epithet of the body; the elementary or vital principle, the proximate cause of life and action; an epithet of Brahmā and of Śiva
s.m. lit. 'Enemy of evil beings'; asafœtida
s.m. 'The refuge of created beings,' an epithet of the Supreme Being
adj. & s.m. Possessed by an evil spirit; — one who is possessed, a maniac
s.m. Possession by an evil spirit
v.n. To remove the influence of an evil spirit (from), to exorcise
v.n. To become a devil, be like a devil; to be intoxicated: bhūt-pret, s.m. Ghosts and goblins
s.f. Creation or origination of existing things
s.m. Possession by an evil spirit
s.m. lit. 'Devil's dish'; (met.) ill-gotten wealth, &c. (soon spent or lost)
s.m. (in Gram.), Past time, past tense
adj. Appertaining to past time, or to past tense
adj. & s.m. Possessed by an evil spirit; — a maniac
s.m. The aggregate or whole collection of beings; the troop or whole class of demons or spirits
s.m. lit. 'Destroying or removing evil spirits'; garlic: a camel
s.m. 'Lord of beings or spirits'; an epithet of Śiva
s.m. lit. 'Destroying evil beings'; the marking-nut plant, Semicarpus anacardium; the berry or seed of the Eleocarpus (used for rosaries)
s.m. lit. 'Abode of evil beings'; the tree Belleric myrobalan, Terminalia bellerica (the nuts of which are used as dice)
s.m. lit. 'Demon-tree'; the tree Trophis aspera; the tree Bignonia indica
s.f. Possession by an evil spirit; epilepsy
s.m. Name of an ancient race of people
s.f. A white flowering Vitex negundo

(bhūta+īśvara), s.m. Lord of beings; lord of evil beings, an epithet of Śiva; name of a town and temple situated between Agra and Etawah, on the banks of the Jamnā, being one of the sacred places of the Hindūs
v.n. To cling (to one) like a devil or a ghost
v.n. To become a devil, to become mad or furious with rage, be distracted with rage.

def: adj. = bhutahā, q.v.
adj. Pos sessed by an evil spirit; demoniacal, devilish; fierce; peevish (also, and more correctly, written bhutāhā).
s.m. See s.v. bhūt.

s.f. State of being, existence, production, birth; pure and undisturbed existence (free from the influence of matter and passion); power, dignity; prosperity, wealth; superhuman power as attainable by the practice of austerity and magical rites; ashes; fried meat; plunder, booty; rut of elephants.
s.f. Female demon or goblin, &c. = bhutnī, q.v.
v.n. To be like a devil; to become exceedingly fierce; to get into a fiendish passion.
s.m. Small devil; imp; devil, demon, goblin, ghost.
s.f. Wife of a devil; female demon; hag; a very ugly woman.
adj. = bhoṅthrā, bhonṭā, qq.v.
s.m. = bhuṭṭā, q.v.
s.m. Spike or ear of (ripe) corn or grain; Indian corn, maize, Zea mays
subs: adj. Like an ear of corn; smallish, very small, stumpy
bhuṭṭā-jā-paṛnā, v.n. To fly off like an ear of corn (struck with a stick), to be cut clean off.

def: s.m. = bhuṭṭā, q.v.
s.m. Blockhead, fool (cf. bhoṅdū; bhonṭā).
s.m. Heaven, sky, ether, atmosphere.
s.m. = bhuvas; bhuva, qq.v.
s.m. = bhū-pāl, q.v.s.v. bhū.
s.m. Being, animated being, living creature; man, mankind; the world; heaven; earth; water; one of the three mundane regions (viz. earth, sky, or the intervening space)
subs: s.f. 'Enchantress of mankind'; an epithet of Māyā: a charming or fascinating woman

def: s.m. Heaven, sky, ether, atmosphere (the word forms part of the mystical triverbial phrase, bhūr, bhuvas, swar, uttered by Brāhmaṇs in commencing their daily prayers).
s.f. Earth, ground ( = bhūmi; pr̤ithvī); — adv. On the ground; in or on the earth.
adv. Further, more, again; repeatedly; afterwards.
s.m. Holding by military service; chief, headman of a Gonḍ village ( = bhoʼī).
adv. Again and again, repeatedly.
v.t. To study, &c. = abhyāsnā; see abhyās.

prep. By, with, from, in, into; near, on, according to, for, towards. (In Urdū it occurs only in comp. with a substantive defined by the Arabic article al, 'the'; e.g. biʼl-lāh, By God; biʼl-ittifāq, adv. By chance, &c.)

ety: fem. of be; cf. re, rī
def: particle (fem.) used in addressing females (placed after the noun: — see next).
conj. contrac. of bhī, q.v.
s.f. Lady.
s.m. Seed.
adv. The very same; exactly, precisely, verbatim.
s.m. Hole (of a snake, rat, &c.).
s.f. Evening.
s.f. Seed-bed, seed-plot.
s.f. = bīʼaṛ, q.v.
adv. By its origin or nature; essentially.
adv. With the aid (of), by the assistance (of).
s.m. Argument, discussion, debate, controversy, contest, dispute, altercation, quarrel; lawsuit
subs: v.n. To raise an objection; to get up a controversy, &c. = bibād karnā, q.v.
s.m. Disputed possession; disputed estate or land
v.n. To debate, dispute; to wrangle, quarrel; to litigate.
wety: Semi-learned borrowing from Sanskrit विवाद (vivādá). Doublet of وِواد (vivād)

def: adj. & s.m. Contentious, quarrelsome, litigious; — disputant; litigant; plaintiff.
s.m. Marriage = byāh, q.v.
s.m. Description, explanation, exposition, interpretation
subs: (-kā), To give a description (of), to describe, &c.

def: adj. Wanting colour, colourless, faded in colour, pale; discoloured.
s.m. = H ببيکتا बिबेकता bibekatā [S. विवेकता], s.f. Discrimination, discernment, judgment, discretion; sense, intelligence, true knowledge.
s.f. = H ببيك बिबेक bibek [S. विवेक], s.m. Discrimination, discernment, judgment, discretion; sense, intelligence, true knowledge.
adj. & s.m. Discriminating, discriminative, discerning, judicious, prudent, discreet, sensible, just, fair, upright; — judge, discriminator, sage, philosopher.
s.m. Part, portion, &c. = vi-bhāg, q.v.
s.f. Part, share, portion, partition; (in Gram.) affix of declension or inflection, case-sign; declension, inflection.
s.f. Name of a rāginī or musical mode.
s.m. Power, might; superhuman power or influence; substance, thing, property, wealth, prosperity.
s.m. Power, might; superhuman power or influence; substance, thing, property, wealth, prosperity.
s.f. Lady, dame, madam; wife; mistress; a term of endearment
subs: bībī ṣāḥib, intj. (A respectful form of address), Lady! Madam!

def: s.f. Lady, &c. (see bībī); the queen (at cards).
adj. Belonging to women, female (applied to Europeans).
adv. = bīć, q.v. (used chiefly in comp.).

s.m. Middle, midst, centre; difference, interval; room, space; average, mean; — quarrel, hostility; — prep. & postp. (governing the gen. with -ke), in the midst (of), in, into; between, among; during; meanwhile
subs: bīćam-bīć, bīćoṅ-bīć, s.m. & adv. The very middle, the very midst; — in the very midst (of); in the exact middle or centre
s.m. Interposition, intervention, mediation, intercession; arbitration, settlement, reconciliation ( = bić-bićāʼo)
(-meṅ), To interpose, mediate; to intercede; to arbitrate, settle, adjust, reconcile, pacify
s.f. Parallel lines (syn. samānāntar rekhā; ḵẖut̤ūt̤-i-mutawāzī)
s.f. Middle language or dialect; mixture of Urdū and Hindī
v.n. To differ; to raise a quarrel between
bīć-meṅ paṛnā, v.n. To interpose, intervene, interfere; to negotiate (in a bargain, marriage, &c.); to be surety or security (for)
adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Situated in the middle or between; — the middle one, &c. ( = bićauliyā, q.v.)

def: s.m. Mask, visor.
= bhićak, q.v.
v.n. To be alarmed, take fright ( = bijhaknā and bhićaknā); to run away, retreat, slink off; to recede or withdraw (from), back out (of), shrink (from = bićalnā, q.v.); to be sprained.
s.f. Straw (cf. bićhānā, 'to spread'; bićhaunā, 'bedding').

v.n. To move about, move to and fro; to roam, wander, ramble; to slip, shift, move out of place or away; to go astray, lose (one's) way; to be lost or missing; to be left behind; to be separated, parted, cut off ( = bićhaṛnā, q.v.); to retreat, draw back; to withdraw or back out (or a promise, &c.); to get out of order, be deranged, spoiled, or marred; to get out of harmony or tune; to get out of temper, become troublesome (as a disappointed child): — bićal-jānā = bićalnā.

s.m. Consideration, reflection, meditation, deliberation, investigation, examination, trial, disputation, discussion, dispute; exercise of judgment or reason, judgment, discrimination, prudence; decision, determination; opinion, thought, apprehension, will
adj. & s.m. = bićārā, be-ćāra, q.q.v.
adj. (f. -ī), Helpless, forlorn; — helpless person; poor creature, poor wretch (used in pity).
adj. & s.m. Deliberating; investigating; discussing; judging; — investigator, discriminator, inspector, judge.
part, Reflecting, thinking.
s.m. = bićārak, q.v.
part. Thought upon, deliberated upon, considered, meditated; decided, determined; &c.
adv. Upon or after due consideration or investigation, &c.; thoughtfully, deliberately, considerately, &c.

ety: bićār˚ = Prk. विचारे(इ) or विचार(इ) = S. विचारय(ति), rt. वि+चर्
def: v.n. and t. To think, deem, opine, conceive, apprehend; to consider, reflect, ponder, deliberate; to comprehend; to examine, discriminate, judge, investigate, estimate, calculate; to think of, resolve on, determine, decide, pronounce judgment on; to feel (pity, fear, &c.).
v.t. = bićalnā, and bićhaṛnā, q.q.v.
s.m. = bićārak, q.v.
part. adj. To be deliberated, weighed or discussed; requiring deliberation, &c.; fit and proper to be discussed.
s.m. One who interposes; one who interferes or intermeddles; interposer; intermeddler; go-between; middle-man, ambassador, agent, broker; matchmaker (syn. bīć-wālā, tisrait).
s.m. = bīć-bićāʼo, q.v.s.v. bīć.
s.m. Intermediate space or distance, interval; interim; difference (syn. antar, fāṣila; farq).
s.f. Scorpion = biććhū, q.v.
s.m. Scorpion; the sign Scorpio of the zodiac; a kind of firework (also written biććhī).
s.m. = biććhū, q.v.; — and = bis, bish, bikh, q.q.v.
s.m. = biććhū, q.v.
adj. (f. -ī), Slippery.
v.n. = bićhaṛnā, q.v.
v.t. To spread, spread out, extend, lay on; to make (a bed); to strew, scatter; to lay prostrate or flat, knock down, fell, lay low ( = bićhā-denā, the more common form in these senses).
s.m. Carpet, bed, &c. = bićhhaunā, q.v.
v.n. = bićhaṛnā, q.v.
v.n. To be separated, parted, divided, &c.; to separate, part, go asunder; to be sprained; to turn away or aside (from); to slip, slide ( = bićalnā, q.v.).
s.m. The stinging nettle, Urtica interrupta (syn. bićhuṭī, bićhuʼā).
v.n. = bićhaṛnā, q.v.
s.m. Bed, bedding, bed-clothes; carpeting, carpet; the carpet on which (Hindu) women sit and bewail the death of a person; (hence, among Hindūs), prescribed period of wailing or lamentation
subs: To open or spread out bedding, &c.; to make (one's) bed; to lay a carpet, &c.
To make a bed; — to lay prostrate or flat, knock down, fell ( = bićhā-denā).

def: s.f. dim. of bićhaunā, q.v.
s.m. Carpet, bed, &c. = bićhhaunā, q.v.
s.m. Carpet, bed, &c. = bićhhaunā, q.v.
s.f. Ornament or ring worn on the toe ( = bićhuʼā, q.v.).
v.n. To be spread, be laid out; to be floored; to become prostrate, lie flat; to humble oneself; to be brought or laid low.
s.m. = bićhohā, q.v.
s.m. Separation, parting; absence.
s.m. Separation, parting; absence.
s.m. Distribution of an aggregate sum among several individuals.

ety: fr. S. विचृत्; or caus. of bićhuṛnā
def: v.t. To detach, disconnect, separate, part, divide, sunder; to pull or pick to pieces; to card (cotton).
s.m. = bićhohā, q.v.
s.f. = bićhṛahaṭ, q.v.
s.f. = H بچهڙاو बिछड़ाव bićhṛāʼo, s.m. = H بچهڙت बिछड़त bićhṛat, s.f. (v.n. from bićhṛānā), Separating, parting, dividing; separation ( = bićhoh); turning away; alienation.
v.t. To cause to be separated; to separate, part, divide, sunder; to scatter, disperse ( = bithrānā).
v.t. = bićhṛāna, q.v.
s.m. = H بچهڙاهٿ बिछड़ाहत bićhṛāhat, s.f. = H بچهڙت बिछड़त bićhṛat, s.f. (v.n. from bićhṛānā), Separating, parting, dividing; separation ( = bićhoh); turning away; alienation.
s.f. = bićhṛāhaṭ, q.v.
s.f. = H بچهڙاو बिछड़ाव bićhṛāʼo, s.m. = H بچهڙاهٿ बिछड़ाहत bićhṛāhat, s.f. (v.n. from bićhṛānā), Separating, parting, dividing; separation ( = bićhoh); turning away; alienation.
s.m. = biććhū, q.v.

s.m. Scorpion ( = biććhū, q.v.); — a kind of dagger; a small stiletto with a curved blade; an iron claw or hook; a ring worn on the (little or great) toe (these various significations all arise from the shape of the dagger, &c. resembling that of a scorpion's sting); — the hemp plant, Crotalaria juncea (syn. san); the seed-pod of the same plant; — the stinging nettle, Urtica heterophylla; also Urtica interrupta ( = bićhātā and bićhuṭī).
s.f. Ornament or ring worn on the toe ( = bićhuʼā, q.v.).

ety: S. वृश्र्च, or H. biććhū+dim. aff. ṭī
def: s.f. Stinging-nettle ( = bićhātā, and bićhuʼa).
s.m. One who sweeps, sweeper.
v.t. To cause to be spread, to have (a carpet, &c.) spread or laid down; to spread ( = bichānā); to cause to spread.
adj. Variegated; various, diverse; surprising, wonderful, rare; admirable; pleasing.
v.t. To alarm, frighten ( = bijhkānā, bhićkānā, q.v.); to cause to retreat or draw back; to disappoint, balk; to break a word or promise.
s.m. The middle finger; — an ornament worn in the anterior lobe of the ear; ear-ring.
s.f. A kind of ear-ring ( = bićkannā).
adj. (f. -ī), Intervening, intermediate, middle; middling ( = bīć-kā); — s.m. An intermediate thing (of three); the middle one of three children (syn. manjhlā).
s.m. Bed for growing plants to be transplanted.
v.t. To pull or pick to pieces; to open out, undo, separate; to break up.
s.m. = bićhuʼā, q.v.
s.m. One who interposes; one who interferes or intermeddles; interposer; intermeddler; go-between; middle-man, ambassador, agent, broker; matchmaker (syn. bīć-wālā, tisrait).
s.m. = bićauliyā, q.v.
s.m. = bićwānī, &c. and bićauliyā, q.v.
s.m. One who interposes; one who interferes or intermeddles; interposer; intermeddler; go-between; middle-man, ambassador, agent, broker; matchmaker (syn. bīć-wālā, tisrait).
s.m. One who interposes; one who interferes or intermeddles; interposer; intermeddler; go-between; middle-man, ambassador, agent, broker; matchmaker (syn. bīć-wālā, tisrait).
s.m. = bićauliyā, q.v.
s.m. = bićwānī, &c. and bićauliyā, q.v.
s.f. A small hole or pit into which children in play endeavour to throw balls or marbles.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian بد (bad). Unrelated to English bad.

def: s.f. = bīdh, q.v.
s.f. Permission to depart, dismission, taking leave; departure; farewell
subs: (-ko), To dismiss, permit (one) to depart; to bid farewell; to see (one) off
v.n. To take leave, say farewell, depart; to depart this life, to die.

def: s.m. Mound; cluster of mounds.
s.f. Presents made by a host to a visitor, or to a servant, on dismissing him; dismissal; departure ( = bidā).
v.t. To turn the plough over a field after the seed is come up; to plough immediately after sowing for the purpose of covering the seed; to harrow.
s.f. Presents made by a host to a visitor, or to a servant, on dismissing him; dismissal; departure ( = bidā).
v.n. To be scared; to start, shy, &c. ( = bijhaknā, q.v.); — to move, stir; to move aside or out of the way; to shift; to draw back, shrink (from); to be dissuaded.
s.m. A kind of harrow (worked by oxen to loosen the soil and remove weeds from the standing crops of young rice).
s.m. A vessel of mixed metal (particularly bharat, q.v.) plated with silver by beating; plating metal with silver by beating (the name is said to be derived from the town of Bidar in the Dakkhan)
subs: s.m. A manufacturer of vessels described above, one who plates metal (especially bharat) with silver.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian بدر (ba-dar), from به (ba) + در (dar).

def: s.m. Tearing, rending; breaking, cleaving, splitting, severing, dividing.
v.t. To tear, rend, &c. (see bidāran).

ety: biḍār˚ = Prk. विडारे(इ) or विडार(इ) = S. विदारय(ति), caus. of rt. वि+दॄ
def: v.t. To drive away, turn away, turn out, expel, exclude; to efface.

ety: biḍar˚ = Prk. विडर(इ) = S. विदृणा(ति), rt. वि+ दॄ; — cf. ḍālnā
def: v.n. = bitharnā; and v.t. = bithrānā; q.q.v.
s.f. Novelty or innovation in religion, schism, heresy; wrong-doing, wrong, violence, oppression, outrage; strife, contention, quarrel
subs: (-par), To do violence or wrong (to); to practise oppression (on); to contend, quarrel.

def: s.m. Innovator in religion; heretic, schismatic; wrong-doer; oppressor, tyrant; contentious or quarrelsome person.
s.f. Presents made by a host to a visitor, or to a servant, on dismissing him; dismissal; departure ( = bidā).
s.m. Another country, foreign land; — adv. To another country, abroad (syn. pardes).
adj. & s.m. Of another country, from foreign parts; foreign; wretched; — foreigner; traveller; outsider, stranger.
s.f. A (Hindū) marriage ceremony in which turmeric, salt, &c. are ground on the seventh or the third day before the wedding.
s.m. Determination of the amount to be paid as government revenue, settlement (syn. band-o-bast).
s.m. Sending; ordering; arrangement, disposition; creation; ordinance, injunction, &c. ( = bidhi, q.v.); worship, ceremony; gaining, gain; wealth; act of hostility; conflict of opposite feelings.
s.m. Arranger, disposer, maker, creator; providence; name of Brahmā (as creator of the world); — fate, destiny (syn. bidhnā).
s.m. = bidhātā, q.v.
s.f. Widow.
s.f. Piercing, perforation.
v.t. To reduce, lessen, diminish, abate.

s.f. Rule, form, formula, sacred precept; precept, maxim; injunction, order, command; law, statute, ordinance; direction (for the performance of a rite), sacred text or scripture prescribing any act or observance; any prescribed act, rite, or ceremony; act, action; behaviour, conduct, mode of life; method, manner, mode, way, means; kind, sort; fate, destiny, lot; — balance of an account; adjustment of an account; — s.m. Name of the god Brahmā; of the god Vishnu
subs: bidh milnā, v.n. To agree, correspond, tally; an agreement to be come to; bargain to be struck

To consult sacred texts with respect to performing a rite or ceremony (as a marriage, &c.); to consult the horoscope of the bride and bridegroom before performing the marriage ceremony; — to check or clear an account; to strike a balance
adj. According to rule, law, precept, &c.

def: v.n. To be pierced or perforated (see bedhnā); to be wounded.
s.m. Arranger, disposer, &c. = bidhātā, q.v.
s.m. The moon; — name of Brahmā; name of Vishṇu.
s.m. Falling to ruin, ruin, destruction, annihilation, extermination.
part. adj. Known, familiar; famed, famous, renowned.
v.t. To scare; to startle, &c. ( = bijhkānā, q.v.); to cause to recede or draw back; to dissuade; to alienate, estrange.
To open out, lay open; to expose; to screw; to expose to ridicule; to laugh at, mock, ridicule.
To open out, lay open; to expose; to screw; to expose to ridicule; to laugh at, mock, ridicule.
s.f. Mixed metal (composed of copper, zinc, and tin) plated with silver (see bidar: It is used to make cups and other vessels, and ḥuqqā bottoms); mixed metal (syn. bharat).
prep. Without, deprived of; with the exception of, besides.
adj. Learned, instructed, intelligent, wise, scientific.

s.f. Learning, science; knowledge; intellect; skill, art; a magical pill or bolus (which placed in the mouth is supposed to give the power of ascending to heaven); magical knowledge or skill; — name of a small insect (called also Saraswatī)
subs: s.m. A particular kind of good or evil genius attending upon the gods; — a learned man; one skilled in magical knowledge
(vidyā+arthī), s.m. Seeker after knowledge, student, scholar, pupil (syn. t̤ālib-ʻilm)
s.m. Abode of learning, school, college.

def: adj. & s.m. Learned, scientific, skilled; a learned man, a scholar ( = bidwān, vidwān).
s.m. = bīghā, q.v.
s.f. Rent fixed on lands per bīghā; — adv. per bīghā ( = bighoṭī; bīghoṭī, q.v.).
adj. Strange, &c. = be-gāna, q.v.
adj. Strange, &c. = be-gāna, q.v.
s.f. Strangeness, &c. = be-gānagī, q.v.
s.f. Bad smell, stench, stink, fetor (syn. bad-bo).
s.m. = begār, q.v.

s.m. Change for the worse, impairment, deterioration, corruption, vitiation, disfigurement, defacement; decline, decay; disorder, disarrangement, disturbance, confusion; ruin, violation, break, smash; harm, injury, damage; defect, blemish, stain, flaw; difference, disagreement, discord, misunderstanding, quarrel; defection, rebellion, revolt, mutiny
subs: v.n. To be ready to quarrel; — bigāṛ karnā (-kā), To harm, spoil; to quarrel; to forfeit friendship.

ety: bigāṛ˚ = Prk. विगाढे(इ) or विगाढ(इ) = S. विघाटय(ति), caus. of वि+घट्
def: v.t. To ruin, spoil, mar, vitiate, &c. (see bigāṛ); to bungle; to break a custom; to cause a misunderstanding (between friends); to foul (one's clothes)

ety: bigaṛ˚ = Prk. विगढ(इ) = S. विघट(ते), rt. वि+घट्
def: v.n. To be changed for the worse, to become worse; to be impaired, deteriorated, defaced, disfigured, distorted; to take harm, be damaged, injured, marred, spoiled, corrupted, vitiated, ruined, destroyed; to fall off; to fail, miscarry; to break down; to go or turn bad; to get out of order, be disordered, tossed, disarranged; to be mismanaged, bungled; to be wasted, squandered; to disagree, be at variance, be estranged, to quarrel; to get out of temper, become angry or enraged; to become vicious, wicked, or unruly; to rebel, revolt, mutiny
s.m. Ruiner, spoiler, destroyer.
v.n. = bikasnā, q.v.
s.f. Bad conduct, misdemeanour; badness.
s.m. = bīghā, q.v.
s.m. A land-measure equal to about five-eighths of an acre. (The kaććā bīghā varies from a fourth to a third of an acre)
subs: bīghā-sar, bīghā-wār, bīghe-gail, Rate per bighā.

def: s.m. Obstacle, impediment, hindrance, obstruction, stop, prevention, interruption, opposition; any difficulty or trouble
subs: bighan karnā (-meṅ), To throw obstacles (in the way), to obstruct, hinder, interrupt, &c.

ety: S. विघन; rt. हन् with वि
def: s.m. Killing, slaying; slaughter, havoc
subs: bighan maćānā, v.t. To kill, slay, slaughter, make havoc.

def: s.m. Wolf ( = bik).
v.n. = bigaṛnā, q.v.
s.f. Land measurement; rate per bīghā; — adv. Per bīghā (see bigahṭī).
s.f. & adv. = bigahṭī, q.v.

ety: S. विगानीयं, rt. गै with वि
def: v.t. To decry, speak ill of, blame, reproach, villify, ridicule; to ruin, destroy.
part. adj. (f. bigoʼī), Decried, blamed, &c.; — s.f. Detraction, evil-speaking, scandal; ridicule, satire.
part. (of bigaṛnā) and adj. (f. -ī), Impaired, damaged, spoiled; out of temper, angry, &c. (see bigaṛnā); — bigṛe dil, adj. Rebellious, refractory, mutinous; reckless, wild, desperate; mad, crazy.
s.m. War, battle, quarrel, strife.
v.t. = bigāṛnā: and bikhernā; q.q.v.
s.m. = bigāṛ, q.v.

ety: S. विकृति; or v.n. fr. bigaṛnā
def: s.f. Damage, harm; spoil; misunderstanding (between friends), disagreement; war, battle (cf. bigār).
s.m. One of the rāgs or musical modes.
s.m. = bihāg, q.v.

s.f. A spirit, supposed to tease infants by whispering alternately sad and pleasing things in their ears (which is the cause of their laughing and crying when asleep or awake); — a song of congratulation, &c. ( = badhāwā, q.v.).
s.m. Dawn, day-light, day-break, morning, morrow (syn. bhinsār)
subs: adv. At dawn, at day-break, in the morning, early, soon, betimes.

def: s.m. Seed (for sowing: — syn. bīj; biyā; bīhan).
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀪𑀇𑀡𑀺 (bhaïṇi), from Sanskrit भगिनी (bhaginī). Cognate with Punjabi بَھیْݨ (bhaiṇ).

ety: bihā˚ = bihāy˚ = Prk. विहाअ(इ), or विहाय(इ) = S. विजहा(ति), rt. वि+हा
def: v.n. To spend or pass time.
s.m. (?), Land cut up by a torrent.
adv. See s.v. bihān.
v.n. = bihasnā, q.v.

ety: S. विहार; rt. हृ with वि
def: s.m. Roving for pleasure, wandering; diversion, amusement, recreation, sport, pastime; name of a province in Bengal
subs: v.n. To rove, stroll, &c.; to divert or amuse oneself.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian بَهَار (bahār, “spring”). Doublet of وَسَنْت (vasant) and بَسَنْت (basant).

def: s.m. Seas, bays, gulfs.
s.m. = viharaṇ, q.v.; and see biharnā.
adj. Sportive, frolicsome, gay; — prop. n. Name of Krishṇa; name of a Hindī poet (a proper name generally among Hindūs).
v.n. To rejoice, take pleasure; — v.t. To enjoy, take pleasure in; to rejoice, delight.
v.n. To smile, laugh; to be pleased (syn kaṅsnā).
adj. Agitated, alarmed, overcome with fear or agitation, beside one's self, unable to restrain one's self.
s.f. Quince; (also, in India) guava; — bihī-dāna, s.m. Quince-seed.
s.f. The quince = bihī, q.v.
subs: s.m. Quince seeds (used as a demulcent in certain complaints).

def: s.f. Goodness; the being well or better; welfare.
(bi+hi), With it, by it (see bi).
adj. & s.m. Pardonable; pardon
(imperat. of hilīdan) = biḥil, q.v.
adj. = bihīn, q.v.
adj. Best, most excellent.
s.f. The abode of the blessed, Paradise (syn. baikunṭh)
subs: s.m. Pomegranate; any delicious fruit: bihisht-kī qumrī, s.f. lit. 'Dove of Paradise'; a dancing girl
s.f. Cool, refreshing breeze; delightful climate
adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Paradisiacal; — inhabitant of paradise, celestial being.

def: adj. & s.m. Of or pertaining to paradise; paradisiacal; celestial; — inhabitant of paradise; the blessed; — a Mohammadan water-supplier (vulg. called a bhīstī).
s.m. Privy purse; skin or glove worn by falconers
subs: s.m. Bearer of the privy purse.

def: s.m. One who separates the seeds from cotton; cotton-comber, cotton-carder (syn. dhunyā).
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀯𑀳𑀤𑀺 (vahadi), from Sanskrit वहति (váhati).

def: s.f. Seed-plot, nursery (for plants).
s.m. Thursday.
s.m. Bursting, splitting, rending, tearing, injuring, destroying.
adj. Left, abandoned; apart; foreign, strange.
s.m. = bīj, q.v. (used chiefly in comp.).
s.m. Seed; germ; source, primar. cause, origin; sperma genitale (viri aut mulieris); son; — advance of seed to agriculturists
subs: bīj jamānā, bīj ḍālnā (-kā), To sow seed; to sow the seed (of), to cause, engender
s.m. Advance of seed and food to agriculturists
s.f. Land on which seed does not germinate
v.n. To be utterly destroyed, to become extinct
s.m. Perquisites of the lower classes, consisting of a portion of seed-corn from the field.

def: s.m. = bijār, q.v.
s.m. A forest.
s.f. Portion of seed-corn which the poorer classes are allowed to take from the field.
s.f. Perquisite of a certain allowance of seed-grain.
adj. = bijālā, q.v.
s.m. An armlet = bijāyaṭh, bijauṭhā, q.q.v.
s.m. Ticket tied to goods, or on bags, to mark their contents, price, &c.; list, invoice, inventory, catalogue; assets, effects.
adj. Having seed or grain, seedy; abounding in seed; run to seed.
adj. = vi-jan, q.v.
s.m. Bull; stallion (cf. bijālā); — (met.) a lusty, profligate man.
adj. Seedy; abounding in seed (a soil), full of seed (fruit, &c. = bijār, bijālā, bijailā, qq.v.).
adj. = bījār, q.v.; — s.m. A description of soil in which the cereal grains are generally grown.
(from bīj), A kind of armlet ( = bijāyaṭh).
s.m. Victory, conquest, triumph (syn. jīt; fataḥ)
subs: s.f. 'The victorious tenth'; the tenth of Ásin śukl paksh, the anniversary of Rām's victory over Rāvan; (generally) any tenth day of a lunar fortnight that falls on a Sunday
v.n. To effect or engage in conquest; — v.t. To conquer, subdue, capture, take (a fort, &c.).

def: s.f. Hemp, Cannabis sativa, or the tops of the plant used as a narcotic (syn. bhāng, bhang).
s.m. A kind of ornament worn on the arm, armlet ( = bijaiṭh, bijauṭhā).
adj. & s.m. Conquering; — conqueror, vanquisher.
v.n. To be startled, scared, frightened; to start; to take fright (cf. jhijhaknā, bidakna, bhićaknā).
s.m. A mixture of barley, wheat, millet, grain, and lentils.
s.m. Name of a tribe of Rājpūts in the district of Gorakhpur.
s.m. A scarecrow (syn. ḍarāʼonā).
v.t. To scare, startle, frighten, alarm ( = bhićkānā).
